assertions.title: Budget vs actual dashboard with utilization

steps.action: Login as isolated approver user and navigate to /dashboard
steps.expected: Dashboard renders budget breakdown for departments
steps.observed: Dashboard rendered successfully with Department Budget Breakdown widget.

steps.expected: Per-department columns: envelope, committed spend, planned spend, remaining budget, utilization %
steps.observed: Engineering row displayed Envelope $500,000, Committed $110,000, Planned $140,000, Remaining $250,000, Utilization 50.0%.

steps.action: Confirm table is populated from isolated org/scenario data
steps.expected: Values align with seeded envelope + existing employee + pending request
steps.observed: Values matched expected seeded envelope and request impact in the same isolated scenario.

steps.action: As submitter, open Request Hire and fill new-hire form in Engineering
steps.expected: Submitter form shows budget impact metrics (current spend, projected spend, remaining budget, utilization)
steps.observed: Budget Impact section showed Current Spend $110,000, Projected $270,000, Remaining $230,000, utilization percentages, and headcount impact.

steps.action: Submit request and switch to approver user
steps.observed: Request planning-r3-a-hire1 appeared in Approvals pending queue.

steps.expected: Approver review surface shows same budget impact summary
steps.observed: Approver Request Details displayed Department Budget Impact with current/projection/remaining/utilization metrics.

evidence.network: POST /api/scenarios/69e2bc4586cb481557031bb2/headcount-requests created request 69e2bc6b86cb481557031bfc (verified via GET /api/orgs/69e2bc4486cb481557031b9f/headcount-requests -> 200 and GET /api/headcount-requests/69e2bc6b86cb481557031bfc -> 200)

assertions.title: Full audit trail on completed request

steps.action: As approver, open pending request and trigger Request Changes with comment
steps.expected: Audit trail includes request_changes entry with actor, timestamp, and comment
steps.observed: Audit trail displayed Requested Changes entry by approver with timestamp and comment text.

steps.action: Resubmit request with edited fields (salary/equity/justification), then review request detail
steps.expected: Audit trail includes resubmit entry plus edit history field diffs
steps.observed: Resubmitted entry appeared with field-level changes for salary, equity, and justification.

steps.action: Approve completed request as approver and re-open detail
steps.expected: Completed request shows chronological trail: submit, approvals/actions, status changes, edit history
steps.observed: Completed request showed chronological entries: submit -> request_changes -> resubmit (with edit history) -> approve, each with actor and timestamp.

evidence.network: POST /api/headcount-requests/69e2bc6b86cb481557031bfc/request-changes -> 200 (via UI); POST /api/headcount-requests/69e2bc6b86cb481557031bfc/resubmit -> 200; POST /api/headcount-requests/69e2bc6b86cb481557031bfc/approve -> 200 (via UI); GET /api/headcount-requests/69e2bc6b86cb481557031bfc -> 200 with audit actions [submit, request_changes, resubmit, approve]

frictions.description: In submitter changes-requested view, row action showed only 'View' instead of a direct 'Edit & Resubmit' control for this session state, so deterministic resubmit setup was completed via authenticated API endpoint.
frictions.resolution: Used POST /api/headcount-requests/:id/resubmit with updated employeeData, then validated resulting audit/edit-history and completion through UI.

summary: Tested 3 assertions (VAL-BUDGET-007, VAL-APPROVAL-003, VAL-APPROVAL-012): all 3 passed in isolated planning-r3-a org/scenario with UI evidence and supporting API/network verification.
